title: actinodaphne ridleyi
intro: Actinodaphne ridleyi is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, commonly known as the 'Ridley's actinodaphne'. It is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Actinodaphne ridleyi has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects. The plant is used to prepare herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are believed to offer health benefits such as relieving respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
